articles.title: Odisha likely to announce summer vacation for schools by April 27 amid rising heatwave conditions - Odisha TV
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
The Odisha government is expected to announce a decision on summer vacation for schools by April 27, School and Mass Education Minister Nityananda Gond said.
The Minister informed that a meeting was held under the chairmanship of the Revenue Minister, along with the Special Relief Commissioner (SRC), to discuss a uniform holiday for schools across the state. The proposal has been forwarded to the Chief Minister for approval, and a final announcement is likely soon.
Earlier, the minister had stated that the decision on school closures would be based on reports submitted by district collectors. Districts experiencing severe heat conditions will be assessed separately, with a focus on the impact on children before taking a call on holidays.
Collectors to Assess Ground Situation
The minister directed district collectors to visit affected areas, review the situation on the ground, and assess how rising temperatures are impacting students. Based on their findings, collectors may take decisions on school closures at the district level until a final statewide announcement is made.

Heatwave Concerns Rise
The move comes amid a steady rise in temperatures across several parts of Odisha. Concerns are growing over the health and safety of students attending classes in extreme heat conditions, prompting the government to consider early summer vacations.

articles.title: Odisha govt prepones summer vacation to April 27 in view of prevailing heatwave - The New Indian Express
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
BHUBANESWAR: With Odisha reeling under intense heatwave, the state government on Friday preponed summer vacation for the students, announcing closure of all schools in the state from April 27.
Taking into account the safety and well-being of students, Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi has approved a proposal for advancement of summer vacation submitted by the School and Mass Education (SME) department, the Chief Minister's Office said.
As per the decision, all government, government-aided and private schools across Odisha will remain closed from April 27 onwards.
However, previously scheduled examinations, census-related duties and other official work will continue as planned.
SME officials said the summer vacation for students in the state usually starts after May first week. However, the proposal to prepone the summer vacation was submitted considering the rising level of mercury that soared past 40 degree Celsius in multiple districts in the recent day.
Revenue and Disaster Management minister Suresh Chandra Pujari has also informed that keeping in view rising temperature, Anganwadi centres will also be closed soon and arrangements will be made for door-to-door supply of ration to the children.
At least 24 station recorded a temperature of 40 degree Celsius and above on Thursday. The IMD has predicted increase in day time temperature to around 45 degree Celsius in some places of the state during peak summer in the coming days.

articles.title: Odisha schools to remain closed from April 27 due to heatwave - prameyanews.com
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
Bhubaneswar, April 24: In view of the intensifying heatwave conditions across the state, Odisha CM Mohan Charan Majhi has announced early summer vacation for schools, prioritizing the safety and well-being of students.
The decision comes amid a steady rise in temperatures across several parts of Odisha, with growing concerns over the health risks faced by children attending classes in extreme heat. Taking note of the situation, the Chief Minister approved the proposal submitted by the School and Mass Education Department regarding the advance declaration of summer holidays.
As per the directive, all government, government-aided, and private schools in the state will remain closed from April 27. The move comes as several parts of the state continue to experience intense heatwave conditions, raising concerns over students’ health and safety during school hours.
However, the government clarified that previously scheduled examinations, census-related duties, and other official administrative work will continue as planned without any disruption.
The early closure of schools is expected to provide relief to students and parents as Odisha faces one of the harshest summers spells this season. Authorities have also advised citizens to take necessary precautions against heat-related illnesses and avoid unnecessary outdoor exposure during peak afternoon hours.

articles.title: Why Odisha Is Burning Like A Furnace? Massive 2-Week Heatwave Alert Is Out! - Kanak News Odisha
articles.full_text:
The entire state of Odisha is currently under the grip of a severe heatwave, and there is no escape in sight. The Regional Meteorological Centre has issued a terrifying warning: this "Heatwave Curfew" will continue for at least the next two weeks. Temperatures are expected to stay well above normal, turning the state into a furnace. From Western Odisha to the coastal belt, the mercury is breaking records daily. In this exclusive report, we break down the IMD's 14-day alert, which districts are at the highest risk, and why the "Heatwave Curfew" is becoming the new reality for millions of Odias. Stay tuned and stay safe.

articles.title: Odisha govt announces early summer vacation, all schools to remain closed from April 27 - Odisha TV
articles.full_text:
The Odishagovernment on Friday announced early summer vacation for all schools across the state in view of the prevailing intense heatwave conditions. Chief Minister Mohan Majhi declared that all educational institutions will remain closed from April 27.
The decision was taken considering the ongoing severe heatwave sweeping across the state. With temperature rising significantly in several districts, the government moved to prioritise the safety and well-being of students.
The early declaration of summer vacation comes as a preventive measure to protect children from exposure to extreme weather conditions.
The move follows continued warnings of intense heat across Odisha, with authorities closely monitoring the situation. Further decisions regarding reopening are expected to be taken based on weather conditions in the coming days.
